notes:
**Your appearance settings have been reset.** This release replaces the theming system. Your previous appearance choices are cleared and the app starts on **Paper · Auto · Cozy** — pick a new theme from Settings → Appearance. (This one-time reset is deliberate; the old settings do not carry over.)

**New**

- **Curated themes.** Appearance is now a single theme picker where every theme is a complete, pre-vetted look — colours, background, and material together. Seven ship: **Paper** (default), **Parchment**, **Slate**, **Violet**, **Aurora** (a soft image background), **Glass** (macOS), and **High Contrast** (AAA). Every theme has proper light and dark variants.
- **Three controls, that's it.** Theme, Mode (Light / Dark / Auto — Auto follows your system live), and Text density (Compact / Cozy / Comfortable). The sliders, backdrop grid, accent picker, and presets are gone — legibility is the app's job now, not a set of dials you have to tune.
- **Native glass on macOS.** The Glass theme lets your desktop show through the sidebars using the real macOS window material — not a CSS blur — so it's crisp and cheap. Text stays fully opaque at all times, and it honours "Reduce transparency" automatically. Glass is macOS-only for now; on other platforms it falls back to a solid look.

**Fixes & polish**

- Every theme is contrast-checked in CI (headings, body, and metadata all clear their legibility targets in both light and dark), so no theme can ship hard to read.

**Under the hood**

- The appearance engine is a ground-up rewrite around a tokens-only theme format; `backdrop-filter` is banned app-wide in favour of native materials; theme switching swaps CSS variables on one element with no reflow. (PRD Addendum 24; ADR-0016. Supersedes the 0.5.0-era composable appearance system.)
